data.title: Micron Stock vs SK Hynix Stock: The Arrival of Micron's HBM4 Could Threaten SK Hynix's Market Lead
data.description:
The race to power the world's most advanced artificial intelligence is intensifying. While Micron Technology (MU) remains the only major memory maker based in the U.S., it is facing a fierce challenge from its biggest rival, South Korea's SK Hynix (HXSCL). Both companies have locked in their production for the entire year, leaving tech giants scrambling to secure the chips they need for the next phase of AI.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade NVDA with leverage
  • New trading tool for NVDA bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade NVDA

SK Hynix Leads the High-Bandwidth Market

SK Hynix currently holds the top spot in the high-bandwidth memory (HBM) market, controlling roughly 62% of the global supply. The company's deep partnership with Nvidia (NVDA) has made it the primary provider for the current generation of AI chips.

Yesterday, SK Hynix Chairman Chey Tae-won noted that the world is in a "structural shortage" that could last until 2030. To stay ahead, the firm is spending $15 billion on new facilities in Yongin, South Korea, to speed up the production of its next-generation HBM4 chips.

Micron Brings Higher Efficiency to AI Servers

Even though Micron has a smaller market share of about 21%, it is winning over customers with better efficiency. Micron's latest HBM3E chips use 30% less power than those of its competitors. This is a huge deal for companies running massive data centers where electricity costs are a major concern.

Micron is also moving fast on its own HBM4 technology. CEO Sanjay Mehrotra confirmed today that Micron has "pre-sold every single HBM4 chip it can make this year." The company is also breaking ground on its massive new New York campus this year to ensure it can keep up with the "RAMageddon" shortage affecting the industry.

Samsung Creates a Three-Way Fight for 2026

While Micron and SK Hynix are the current favorites, Samsung (SSNLF) is making a major comeback. After falling into third place last year, Samsung recently cleared quality tests for its own HBM4 chips.

Samsung just secured a massive deal to supply up to 800 million gigabits of HBM4 memory to OpenAI for its custom Titan AI processor. A focus on these new custom chips allows Samsung to aim for over 30% of the market by the end of 2026.

AI Squeeze Raises Prices for Everyone

Because these three giants are focusing all their energy on AI chips, there are fewer regular memory chips being made for everyday gadgets. This shift has caused the price of standard PC and smartphone memory to jump by more than 40% in the first quarter of 2026 alone. Some experts are even calling this period "RAMageddon" because of how hard it is to find affordable parts.

To protect themselves, companies like Apple (AAPL) are now signing five-year deals with Micron and Samsung to make sure they don't run out of memory for their future devices.

Is Micron a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to TipRanks, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Micron stock based on 25 Buy and two Hold recommendations. The average 12-month MU stock price target of $537.57 indicates 35% upside potential.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Nintendo Co Stock (NTDOF) Plummets on Switch 2 Production Cut
data.description:
Nintendo Co (NTDOF) stock plummeted on Tuesday following reports that the video game company had reduced its production output for the Switch 2. According to these reports, Nintendo dropped Switch 2 output to 4 million this quarter, compared to its prior plan to produce 6 million units. The same sources also claim that this reduced output will continue into the next quarter.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nintendo's decision to reportedly reduce its Switch 2 production is related to the console's poor holiday performance and a lack of interest from gamers in the U.S. This is despite what was a strong launch for the console, which became the fastest-selling game console. Now, there's real concern that Nintendo will have trouble growing its Switch 2 user base in the console's second year.

Why Are Switch 2 Sales In Trouble?

Nintendo has faced several issues with the Switch 2 since its release, and not all of them are the company's fault. The ongoing trade war caused price and supply issues. The AI boom has only made that worse with memory prices on the rise.

Additionally, gamers weren't receptive to the company's price increase for games. Nintendo now has an MSRP of $70 for Switch 2 games and has boosted that to $80 for select titles, such as Mario Kart World. For comparison, Switch games were priced at $60, with a couple of games that had $70 price tags.

Nintendo Stock Dives Today

Nintendo stock was down 9.3% on Tuesday, extending a 15.73% year-to-date fall. The shares have also decreased 12.69% over the past 12 months.
data.author: William White

data.title: U.S. Business Activity Slips to 11-Month Low as Iran War Reignites Inflation Fears
data.description:
The impact of the U.S.-Iran war is starting to surface in economic data, with the S&P Global Flash Composite Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) falling to an 11-month low.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

In March, the Flash Composite PMI fell to 51.4 from 51.9 in February, dragged down by input prices rising at the highest pace in 10 months. This also contributed to the largest increase in average selling prices since August 2022. A PMI reading above 50 indicates economic expansion, while a reading below 50 indicates contraction.

War-Driven Costs Push Inflation, Threaten Growth

Higher prices were largely driven by surging energy costs and supply disruptions caused by the war. "Companies are reporting a hit to demand from the additional uncertainty and cost of living impact generated by the conflict," said Chris Williamson, Chief Business Economist at S&P Global Market Intelligence.

Williamson added that the travel, transport, and tourism sectors are especially vulnerable to the war, which could lead to inflation, depressed consumer sentiment, and higher interest rates. The U.S. could also be at risk of stagflation, with the PMI data indicating annualized gross domestic product (GDP) growth of 1% and consumer inflation rising back to the 4% level.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Troubled Canadian Subprime Lender Goeasy (GSY) Delays Its Financial Results
data.description:
More bad news for Goeasy (TSE:GSY) as the troubled Canadian subprime lender has delayed issuing its financial results as it negotiates with its creditors.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Goeasy provides loans and lease-to-own agreements for people who have difficulty accessing traditional banks due to a poor financial history. The company was set to report financial results on March 25 but has now delayed its latest earnings print.

Management said they now expect to release fourth-quarter 2025 results after the market closes on March 31, followed by a conference call with analysts and media.

On Monday, Goeasy announced it has secured covenant waivers from its lenders and financiers, allowing it to remain compliant with agreed terms while booking an expected $331 million in writedowns for the three months ended Dec. 31.

"We are pleased to have reached this outcome with our lenders and other financing counterparties. The facilities … together with the strong cash flow generation of the business, provide goeasy with the liquidity to continue executing on our priorities," Felix Wu, goeasy's chief financial officer, stated in the news release.

Goeasy says the syndicated revolving credit facility will remain at $550 million and mature in July 2027.

Two weeks ago, Goeasy surprised investors by reporting hefty loan losses and suspending its dividend. That news sent shares down by more than 50 per cent on the Toronto Stock Exchange.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Micron Stock (MU) Slides as Rival SK Hynix Eyes a U.S. Listing
data.description:
Micron (MU) stock dipped on Tuesday alongside news that rival memory and storage company SK Hynix (HXSCL) is seeking additional funding. According to recent reports, SK Hynix wants to raise $10 billion from a listing in the U.S. This would help the company with its plan to purchase $8 billion of extreme ultraviolet equipment from ASML Holding (ASML) by the end of 2027.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

If SK Hynix pursues a U.S. listing for its shares, it could be a negative catalyst for Micron stock. As it stands now, investors don't have many options for investing in SK Hynix. The company's stock is only available over-the-counter or through the CSOP SK Hynix Daily 2X Leveraged Product ETF (HK:7709). However, a U.S. listing would make it more easily accessible to investors, who could trade out their shares of MU stock for SK Hynix stock.

Despite the potential increase in competition for Micron, the company did get a vote of investor confidence yesterday. Four-star Phillip Securities analyst Yik Ban Chong reiterated a Buy rating and increased his price target to $530 from $500, implying a possible 35.83% upside for the shares. Chong pointed to a strong earnings report and increased demand for its advanced high‑bandwidth memory for AI GPUs as reasons to remain bullish on MU stock.

Micron Stock Slips Today

Micron stock was down 2.55% on Tuesday but was still up 38.15% year-to-date. The stock has also rallied 329.34% over the past 12 months. Those year-over-year gains make sense, as the company has seen its shares boosted by the need for computer components amid the AI boom.

MU stock trading activity today was low, with about 4.45 million shares traded, compared to a three-month average daily trading volume of about 35.7 million shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: S&P 500 Price Target Boosted to 7,650 by Barclays with 'Bumpy' Road Ahead
data.description:
Barclays raised its 2026 S&P 500 (SPX) price target to 7,650 from 7,400 on Tuesday, implying upside of nearly 17% from current levels. The firm also raised its earnings estimate to $321 per share, up from $305.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

However, the firm warned of near-term volatility driven by the U.S.-Iran war, AI disruptions, and weakness in the private credit market. "We are incrementally bullish ​on US equities, though the road likely stays bumpy until we ​turn a corner," said Barclays Strategist Venu Krishna.

Barclays Sets S&P 500 Bear Case Price Target to 5,900

Barclays set its bear case price target to 5,900, which signals downside of 10% from current prices. The firm noted that rising oil prices could lift inflation and force the Fed into an "unenviable ​corner." It also highlighted growing redemption pressure in private credit funds as a risk that could deepen the downturn if sentiment deteriorates.

Still, Barclays believes that the current cycle in the stock market will be able to overpower any negative catalysts, with technology acting as a "secular growth engine."
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Circle Stock Could Get a Boost on New Nvidia-Backed Africa Deal that Opens 30 New Markets
data.description:
Circle Internet Group (CRCL) stock could get a boost after the blockchain-based financial company announced its first major partnership in Africa. Circle is teaming up with Sasai Fintech, a branch of the Nvidia-backed (NVDA) Cassava Technologies, to bring the USDC (USDC-USD) stablecoin to 30 countries.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade NVDA with leverage
  • New trading tool for NVDA bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade NVDA

USDC Partnership Helps More People Use Digital Money

This deal allows users of the Sasai money-transfer app to use USDC for sending money both locally and across borders. The plan is to help people and businesses deal with local money losing its value or a lack of U.S. dollars in the region.

Strive Masiyiwa, the founder and chairman of Cassava Technologies, said the deal is a big step forward. He noted that "Africa's digital economy is entering a new era," and by adding USDC to the Sasai platform, the partnership will "open up more business opportunities and drive financial inclusion on the continent."

Growing Markets Lead the Way for Digital Dollars

Stablecoins like USDC are becoming popular as a safe way to hold money during global trouble. After the war in Iran started, the total stablecoin market hit a record high of about $316 billion, according to DefiLlama.

Circle Co-Founder and Chairman Jeremy Allaire believes emerging markets are the most important area for growth. He said that "Africa represents a significant opportunity to extend USDC and Circle's infrastructure to high-growth payments corridors." With Africa having the world's youngest and most tech-savvy population, there is a high demand for faster and cheaper ways to move money.

New Rules Could Change How Stablecoins Work

Even with this growth, Circle has to follow new government rules. U.S. lawmakers are working on laws to treat stablecoins like regular payment tools. One change "might stop them from paying automatic interest to holders," which could change how people use them in the future.

Circle is also part of Mastercard's (MA) Crypto Partner Program, showing that major financial players are starting to support the technology. While the stock saw a slight drop this morning, the expansion into Africa creates a new path for the company to reach millions of new users.

Is Circle a Stock to Buy?

Turning to TipRanks, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Circle stock (CRCL) based on 11 Buys, six Holds, and one Sell. The average 12-month CRCL stock price target of $129.11 indicates 3.9% upside potential.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: FinTech360 and TipRanks Partner to Bring Advanced Investment Research to Forex and CFD Brokers Worldwide
data.description:
Fintech360, a leading technology provider of forex brokerage solutions and white label forex trading platforms for the global brokerage industry, is proud to announce a strategic partnership with TipRanks, the world's leading financial research platform for data-driven investment intelligence. 

This collaboration integrates institutional-grade research, analyst insights, and AI-powered analytics directly into the Fintech360 broker ecosystem, empowering forex and CFD brokers to deliver a more sophisticated, value-driven trading experience to their clients. 

Smarter Tools for Smarter trading 

Today’s retail investors demand a platform that executes trades seamlessly and delivers the same quality of research and market intelligence that institutional players rely on. Through this partnership, FinTech360 brokers can now offer their clients access to TipRanks’ comprehensive suite of research tools, enabling confident, well-informed investment decisions at every level of experience. 

TipRanks has long been recognized for bridging the gap between Wall Street and the retail investor. By integrating TipRanks into the FinTech360 ecosystem, brokers gain access to the same powerful data and analytics used by leading financial institutions – now made available to their entire client base. 

What the Partnership Delivers 

The FinTech360-TipRanks integration provides brokers and their clients with a comprehensive, all-in-one investment research suite, including: 

  • Analyst consensus & price target – Clients get an instant, aggregated view of professional analyst ratings and price targets directly within the asset view – eliminating the need to research multiple sources separately. With a clear consensus breakdown and a data-backed price target, traders can make faster, more confident buy/sell decisions without ever leaving the platform. 
  • Smart Score – A single composite score consolidating 8 market signals – including analyst consensus, insider activity, hedge fund trends, news sentiment, technicals, and more – into one clear, actionable indicator rated 1 to 10. Clients can quickly assess the overall health of a stock without manually interpreting multiple data layers, significantly reducing cognitive load and decision time. 
  • Insiders – Clients gain direct visibility into buying and selling activity by corporate executives and directors – information that is typically difficult to track. Displayed as a clear trend with yearly transaction history, this signals how those closest to the company view its near-term prospects, giving traders a valuable confirmatory or contrarian signal before they act. 
  • Hedge fund activity – Clients can see whether major institutional players are increasing or reducing their exposure to a stock, with quarterly holdings data drawn from 487 hedge funds tracked by TipRanks. Monitoring smart money movements helps retail traders align their decisions with market leaders – or spot meaningful divergences early. 
  • Stock market news – Relevant, curated financial news is surfaced directly within the asset view, keeping clients informed without requiring them to leave the platform. This reduces the risk of trading based on outdated information and turns the broker platform into a destination for real-time market intelligence. 

By embedding TipRanks data natively across these five dimensions, FinTech360 delivers a full research-to-execution workflow in one place – reducing platform switching, improving decision quality, and giving brokers a measurable edge in client engagement and retention. 

Uri Gruenbaum, founder and CEO of TipRanks, said, "TipRanks is proud to provide essential datasets and research tools to FinTech360. This partnership empowers brokers and their clients to make smart investing decisions, based on TipRanks' clear presentation of financial data. FinTech 360's complete solution creates a confident trading environment for brokers and their clients, which melds seamlessly with TipRanks' mission of leveling the playing field for all investors."

Building the Broker of Tomorrow 

By integrating TipRanks into its platform, FinTech360 enables brokers to go beyond execution and become true financial partners for their clients. The result is a trading environment where clients have the tools, insights, and confidence to take control of their financial future – and brokers have the competitive edge to stand out in an increasingly crowded market. 

About FinTech360 

FinTech360 is a leading technology provider for the global brokerage industry, offering a complete 360 solution that simplifies operations, accelerates growth, and enhances performance. The ecosystem includes a powerful brokerage CRM, Webtrader, Cashier & Payments Hub, BI suite, Mobile Apps, Marketing platform and an Affiliate Network – all designed to help brokers operate more efficiently, onboard clients faster, reduce costs, and scale with confidence. Trusted by brokers worldwide, FinTech360 combines advanced technology with deep industry expertise to deliver a seamless, reliable, and fully integrated trading infrastructure. 

About TipRanks 

TipRanks is the world’s leading financial research platform, providing retail and institutional investors with data-driven tools to make smarter investment decisions. From analyst ratings and Smart Scores to insider activity and hedge fund data, TipRanks aggregates and analyzes millions of financial data points to deliver actionable intelligence – transparently and objectively.
